items.title: Preprocessor and macros: reject the invalid rule
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this C misconception? A function-like macro behaves exactly like an inline function and evaluates each argument once.
hints: Compare each option with the exact language rule, standard boundary, and observable evidence in the authored draft.

feedback.correct: Correct. Macros are token replacement; parenthesize carefully, avoid side-effecting arguments, and prefer typed functions or constants when possible.
feedback.incorrect: That option preserves the documented misconception. Macros are token replacement; parenthesize carefully, avoid side-effecting arguments, and prefer typed functions or constants when possible.

criteria.description: Selects the source-aligned C correction instead of the documented misconception.

options.text: Macros are token replacement; parenthesize carefully, avoid side-effecting arguments, and prefer typed functions or constants when possible.
A function-like macro behaves exactly like an inline function and evaluates each argument once.

answer.explanation: Macros are token replacement; parenthesize carefully, avoid side-effecting arguments, and prefer typed functions or constants when possible.

items.title: Preprocessor and macros: executable application
items.prompt: Define a fully parenthesized SQUARE macro, read a long long value whose square is representable, and print the square; the task contract forbids side-effecting macro arguments.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted deterministic draft awaiting independent human standards, security, and assessment review.

hints: Begin with the declared input and exact output contract, then implement the named language facet without relying on undefined behavior.

feedback.correct: The reference-aligned visible and hidden deterministic checks passed in the pinned isolated runtime.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic check failed; inspect input validation, boundary behavior, and the named language rule without exposing hidden cases.

criteria.description: Compiles in the pinned runtime and passes every visible and hidden exact-output case.
Uses the target C or C++ language facet with declared bounds and without an out-of-scope workaround.

privateAuthorNotes: AI-assisted and human-unreviewed: formal exam eligibility is prohibited until independent technical and assessment approval.
